chief_complaint: A 32-year-old woman presents with hypertriglyceridemic acute pancreatitis.
history: The patient has a history of 5 pregnancies and 1 live birth. She was transferred from the obstetrics department to the ICU at 29 weeks and 4 days of pregnancy due to hypertriglyceridemic acute pancreatitis.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings are not explicitly stated.
labs: Diagnosing hypertriglyceridemic acute pancreatitis involves investigations such as blood tests to measure triglyceride levels.
